summary: Matched the ft_0D31 death/rebirth area by completing multiple death-state functions, splitting the 0x800D4DD4+ rebirth tail into a new matching ft_0D4D.c unit, and updating build splits, symbols, headers, includes, and shared motion-var typing. The decomp.dev bot reported +6896 matched code bytes, +10644 linked code bytes, and 34 new matches; the apparent broken ft_0D31 matches were primarily functions moved into the new ft_0D4D unit.

key_files.role: Completed/matched death-state code including ftCo_800D3950, ftCo_800D3BC8, ftCo_800D3E40, ftCo_DeadUpStar_Anim, ftCo_800D4580, ftCo_800D481C, and ftCo_DeadUpFall_Anim; moved rebirth tail out.
New matching translation unit for 0x800D4DD4-0x800D5AEC rebirth/dead-up-fall tail functions.
Removed moved rebirth declarations and tightened ftCo_800D481C from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to a typed prototype.
New header for fn_800D4DD4, ftCo_800D4E50, ftCo_800D4FF4, Rebirth, and RebirthWait declarations.
Split ft_0D31 at 0x800D4DD4, added ft_0D4D text/sdata/sdata2 ranges, and shifted following ftCo_Attack100 rodata/sdata ranges.
Renamed local sdata/sdata2 constants from ftCo_804D... aliases to anonymous @NNN labels for the split units.
Changed ft_0D31 from NonMatching to Matching and added ft_0D4D as Matching.
Refined ftCommon_MotionVars.common offsets x4/x8/xC into Vec3 x4.
Updated effect callbacks to the new common.x4 Vec3 layout while preserving integer bit checks on x4.z.
Changed Player_GetUnk45 return type from u8 to u32 to match indexing/codegen used by death-effect color lookup.

what_changed: configure.py now builds melee/ft/ft_0D31.c as Matching instead of NonMatching and adds melee/ft/ft_0D4D.c as a Matching object.
config/GALE01/splits.txt divides ft_0D31.c into text 0x800D3158-0x800D4DD4 plus rodata/sdata/sdata2, then adds ft_0D4D.c for text 0x800D4DD4-0x800D5AEC with its own sdata/sdata2 ranges.

src/melee/ft/ft_0D31.c replaces several /// # nonmatching stubs with C for directional death and dead-up states, including left/right/down/up setup, star/fall animations, effect spawning, sound/camera setup, and dead-up-fall physics store-order fixes.
The rebirth/dead-up-fall tail formerly in ft_0D31.c was moved into new src/melee/ft/ft_0D4D.c with a new ft_0D4D.h header.

ft_0D4D.c contains matched implementations for fn_800D4DD4, ftCo_DeadUpFall_Cam, ftCo_800D4E50, ftCo_800D4F24, ftCo_800D4FF4, ftCo_Rebirth_Anim/IASA/Phys/Coll/Cam, ftCo_800D5600, ftCo_RebirthWait_Anim/IASA/Phys/Coll, and fn_800D5A30.

Dependent files that needed moved rebirth declarations were switched from including ft/ft_0D31.h to ft/ft_0D4D.h, including ftCrazyHand/ftCh_Init.c, ftMasterHand/ftMh_Damage_0.c, ft_0C8C.c, ftdata.c, ftlib.c, ftmaterial.c, and player.c; ftmotionstates.c now includes ft_0D4D.h too.
ftCommon_MotionVars.common changed from separate x4/x8/xC fields to Vec3 x4, and accesses were updated in ft_0C31.c, ft_0D4D.c, and eflib.c.
Player_GetUnk45 now returns u32 instead of u8, matching its use as a shifted offset into Fighter_804D650C/Fighter_804D6508 color arrays.
config/GALE01/symbols.txt updates the affected local sdata and sdata2 constants to anonymous labels such as @403, @404, @290, and @406 instead of ftCo_804D... address-style aliases.
smart_moves: Splitting ft_0D4D into its own translation unit made the build configuration reflect the real code/data boundary and let both ft_0D31 and the moved rebirth functions be marked Matching.
The PR treated the bot's ft_0D31 broken matches as relocation fallout: the same functions reappear as new matches in main/melee/ft/ft_0D4D rather than being actual lost matches.
Modeling common.x4/x8/xC as a Vec3 clarified rebirth target-position math and camera code, while the eflib casts preserved the old integer test on the z slot where codegen required it.
The death-effect color packing was rewritten around Player_GetUnk45(slot) << 2 and explicit RGB assembly from Fighter_804D650C/Fighter_804D6508, matching the generated indexing pattern.
The new ft_0D4D.h kept moved declarations out of ft_0D31.h and let dependent files include the narrower address-range header.
Local data labels were not over-named: symbols.txt switched them to compiler-style @NNN locals where semantic names were not established.
The decomp used targeted codegen controls and artifacts, including #pragma dont_inline, dummy stack arrays, comma expressions, and temporary variables, only where needed to land matches.
decomp_lessons: When a file split is needed for matching, update splits.txt, symbols.txt, configure.py, the new header, and all includes together; otherwise data ranges and apparent match status become misleading.
A bot report can show broken matches in the old unit after a split; verify whether those symbols were re-homed and rematched in the new unit before treating them as regressions.
Adjacent anonymous motion-var fields should be revisited as typed aggregates when multiple functions use them as coordinates; here x4/x8/xC became Vec3 x4.

Do not remove unnatural-looking decomp constructs too early. Constructs such as (0, expr), new_var temporaries, new_var++/--, dummy u8 stack padding, and explicit store order were used to satisfy MWCC codegen.
Use typed prototypes to replace UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS once call sites and argument types are known; ftCo_800D481C became void ftCo_800D481C(Fighter_GObj*, s32).
Bitfield selection matters for matching and semantics: death setup calls changed pl_8003DF44 to use x221F_b4 rather than x221F_b2.
When modeling a field as Vec3, update cross-system users such as effect callbacks and camera code, not only the fighter file that motivated the change.
naming_conventions: New address-range files follow the ft_0D4D.c / ft_0D4D.h pattern for code beginning at 0x800D4DD4.
The new ft_0D4D.h include guard is GALE01_0D4DD4, matching the address-style convention.
Common fighter functions keep the ftCo_ prefix; still-unknown helpers retain fn_800D.... names.
The file-local quaternion is named lbl_803B7500, consistent with address-label naming for unnamed rodata.
Anonymous local data symbols in symbols.txt use @NNN labels rather than invented ftCo_804D... names when no semantic name is justified.
Motion-variable fields kept placeholder names but gained a typed aggregate: mv.co.common.x4 is a Vec3 accessed through .x, .y, and .z.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used #pragma push / #pragma dont_inline on / #pragma pop around ftCo_800D3950, ftCo_800D3BC8, and ftCo_800D3E40 to control inlining.
Used comma expressions such as (0, temp_r28_2->x4), (0, &p_ftCommonData->x504), and (0, (Fighter*) HSD_GObjGetUserData(gobj)) to shape codegen.
Introduced artificial temporaries such as new_var and even new_var++ followed by new_var-- in ftCo_800D41C4 to preserve register/lifetime behavior.
Kept dummy stack storage like u8 _[24], u8 _[8], and PAD_STACK-style padding in matched functions.
Read p_ftCommonData blocks as s32* and cast selected entries to f32 for dead-up timing, velocity, and rotation constants.
Matched store order in ftCo_DeadUpFall_Phys by zeroing x5C.z, then x5C.y, then x5C.x.
Used local scopes around var_r3 and color array base pointers to guide register allocation for efSync_Spawn calls.
Preserved integer bit-pattern tests after the Vec3 refactor with *(s32*) &fighter->mv.co.common.x4.z in eflib callbacks.
review_feedback: No human review comments or reviews were present in the PR slice.
The decomp.dev bot reported for GALE01: matched code 63.28% (+0.18%, +6896 bytes), linked code 34.36% (+0.27%, +10644 bytes), matched data +104 bytes, and linked data +104 bytes.

The bot listed 34 new matches, including ftCo_800D4FF4, ftCo_DeadUpFall_Anim, ftCo_DeadUpStar_Anim, ftCo_800D3950, ftCo_800D3BC8, ftCo_800D3E40, ftCo_800D4580, ftCo_800D481C, and many ft_0D4D rebirth functions.
The bot also listed 15 broken ft_0D31 matches, but the diff shows those rebirth/dead-up-fall tail functions were moved to the new ft_0D4D unit.
follow_up_queries: ft_0D31 ftCo_800D3950 ftCo_800D3BC8 ftCo_800D3E40 efSync_Spawn color1 color2
ft_0D31 ftCo_DeadUpStar_Anim ftCo_DeadUpFall_Anim p_ftCommonData x504 x520
